personas.name: Mara Voss
personas.mandate: requirements schema compliance;W6H completeness;example mapping readiness
questions: Does the requirements document follow the required output schema and section order exactly, including Problem Statement, W6H, Example Mapping, Acceptance Criteria, Out Of Scope, and Open Questions?
Are W6H and example-mapping entries concrete enough for design work without downstream inference?
Are acceptance criteria behavior-focused and free of implementation task leakage?
red_flags: missing required schema sections or unsupported front-matter fields
approved status despite absent W6H or example mapping
implementation-plan decisions embedded in requirements
personas.questions_text: Does the requirements document follow the required output schema and section order exactly, including Problem Statement, W6H, Example Mapping, Acceptance Criteria, Out Of Scope, and Open Questions?;Are W6H and example-mapping entries concrete enough for design work without downstream inference?;Are acceptance criteria behavior-focused and free of implementation task leakage?
personas.red_flags_text: missing required schema sections or unsupported front-matter fields;approved status despite absent W6H or example mapping;implementation-plan decisions embedded in requirements

personas.name: Alistair Sterling
personas.mandate: zero hardcoded roles;Core role neutrality;dog configurability;SDK self-sufficiency
questions: Does any requirement introduce role-conditional logic or hardcoded role assumptions into Core assets or Go business logic?
Is the default dog maintenance agent specified as replaceable pack configuration rather than a Go-side exception?
Can SDK infrastructure still operate with only the controller and Core config, without assuming Gastown agents exist?
red_flags: Core assets or Go code retain Gastown role names outside migration docs or absence tests
dog becomes special-cased in Go rather than user-supplied config
infrastructure behavior depends on a specific configured role existing
personas.questions_text: Does any requirement introduce role-conditional logic or hardcoded role assumptions into Core assets or Go business logic?;Is the default dog maintenance agent specified as replaceable pack configuration rather than a Go-side exception?;Can SDK infrastructure still operate with only the controller and Core config, without assuming Gastown agents exist?
personas.red_flags_text: Core assets or Go code retain Gastown role names outside migration docs or absence tests;dog becomes special-cased in Go rather than user-supplied config;infrastructure behavior depends on a specific configured role existing

personas.name: Oleg Marchetti
personas.mandate: Gastown behavior preservation;before-after inventory;trigger and notification continuity
questions: Does every generalized Core asset have a corresponding external Gastown home for stripped role-specific behavior?
Does the before-and-after inventory cover formulas, orders, scripts, prompts, template variables, and notification paths rather than only file moves?
What artifact proves supported Gastown workflows still resolve and trigger after the split?
red_flags: Gastown behavior is stripped from Core with no equivalent in gascity-packs/gastown
the migration map omits side-effecting scripts, triggers, or notification paths
equivalence is asserted without verifiable before-and-after evidence
personas.questions_text: Does every generalized Core asset have a corresponding external Gastown home for stripped role-specific behavior?;Does the before-and-after inventory cover formulas, orders, scripts, prompts, template variables, and notification paths rather than only file moves?;What artifact proves supported Gastown workflows still resolve and trigger after the split?
personas.red_flags_text: Gastown behavior is stripped from Core with no equivalent in gascity-packs/gastown;the migration map omits side-effecting scripts, triggers, or notification paths;equivalence is asserted without verifiable before-and-after evidence

personas.name: Priya Menon
personas.mandate: required Core loading;pack registry;import resolution;legacy import retirement
questions: How does Core become the canonical required runtime pack across init, doctor, CLI load, and runtime resolution?
Are implicit Maintenance and in-tree examples/gastown imports explicitly retired while gc init --template gastown imports gascity-packs/gastown?
What is the deterministic resolution order when Core, Gastown, bd, and dolt all participate?
red_flags: multiple Core source-of-truth paths remain active
pack resolution falls back to in-tree examples/gastown/packs or implicit Maintenance
required Core behavior is inconsistent between init, doctor, and runtime load
personas.questions_text: How does Core become the canonical required runtime pack across init, doctor, CLI load, and runtime resolution?;Are implicit Maintenance and in-tree examples/gastown imports explicitly retired while gc init --template gastown imports gascity-packs/gastown?;What is the deterministic resolution order when Core, Gastown, bd, and dolt all participate?
personas.red_flags_text: multiple Core source-of-truth paths remain active;pack resolution falls back to in-tree examples/gastown/packs or implicit Maintenance;required Core behavior is inconsistent between init, doctor, and runtime load

personas.name: Petra Novak
personas.mandate: builtinpacks registry;embed path migration;Maintenance retirement;downstream references
questions: When Core moves to its canonical embedded location, are all importers, embed.go files, registry entries, hook code, and generation commands updated together?
Does builtin materialization stop embedding and auto-including retired Maintenance while still materializing Core, bd, and dolt correctly?
Are downstream references to moved Maintenance scripts repointed to Core homes without dangling paths?
red_flags: stale bootstrap or embed paths break compilation or runtime materialization
builtin materialization still auto-includes Maintenance after retirement
examples, dolt helpers, or hooks retain dangling references to moved scripts
personas.questions_text: When Core moves to its canonical embedded location, are all importers, embed.go files, registry entries, hook code, and generation commands updated together?;Does builtin materialization stop embedding and auto-including retired Maintenance while still materializing Core, bd, and dolt correctly?;Are downstream references to moved Maintenance scripts repointed to Core homes without dangling paths?
personas.red_flags_text: stale bootstrap or embed paths break compilation or runtime materialization;builtin materialization still auto-includes Maintenance after retirement;examples, dolt helpers, or hooks retain dangling references to moved scripts

personas.name: Camille Okafor
personas.mandate: existing-city upgrade;legacy local paths;compatibility shims;version skew
questions: For an existing city referencing packs/maintenance or packs/gastown local paths, what exact migration steps and diagnostics does the requirement demand?
Is any compatibility shim or grace behavior scoped and sequenced so pinned or in-flight references degrade gracefully?
How does the rollout handle public pack pinning, intermediate two-repo states, rollback, and operator repair expectations?
red_flags: legacy-path cities fail hard with no actionable migration instruction
compatibility shims become permanent alternate sources of truth
public pack versions drift or require a flag-day multi-repo release
personas.questions_text: For an existing city referencing packs/maintenance or packs/gastown local paths, what exact migration steps and diagnostics does the requirement demand?;Is any compatibility shim or grace behavior scoped and sequenced so pinned or in-flight references degrade gracefully?;How does the rollout handle public pack pinning, intermediate two-repo states, rollback, and operator repair expectations?
personas.red_flags_text: legacy-path cities fail hard with no actionable migration instruction;compatibility shims become permanent alternate sources of truth;public pack versions drift or require a flag-day multi-repo release

personas.name: Faisal Khoury
questions: When resolved config lacks Core or references retired paths, does the diagnostic identify the exact source and explain why Core is required?
Is any offered fix safe, idempotent, and concrete rather than merely advisory prose?
Do doctor and import-state messages consistently distinguish required Core from optional Gastown and retired Maintenance?
red_flags: diagnostics are generic and do not identify the offending config source
fix commands mutate custom config unsafely or are not actually wired
messages keep saying Maintenance is implicit or blur required and optional pack behavior
personas.questions_text: When resolved config lacks Core or references retired paths, does the diagnostic identify the exact source and explain why Core is required?;Is any offered fix safe, idempotent, and concrete rather than merely advisory prose?;Do doctor and import-state messages consistently distinguish required Core from optional Gastown and retired Maintenance?
personas.red_flags_text: diagnostics are generic and do not identify the offending config source;fix commands mutate custom config unsafely or are not actually wired;messages keep saying Maintenance is implicit or blur required and optional pack behavior

personas.name: Hugo Bautista
personas.mandate: asset ownership classification;split assets;review-marked decisions;file-by-file migration table
questions: Does the requirement define complete ownership for Core, Gastown, split, retired, and review-marked assets at file-by-file granularity?
For split assets such as dispatch skills, maintenance docs, architecture fragments, following-mol, command glossary, and TDD discipline content, is each resulting Core and Gastown output explicit?
Are review-marked assets resolved before downstream implementation depends on them?
red_flags: large asset buckets are moved wholesale without behavior-based classification
specific review-marked assets remain unresolved
the migration table omits rationale or leaves orphaned duplicated behavior
personas.questions_text: Does the requirement define complete ownership for Core, Gastown, split, retired, and review-marked assets at file-by-file granularity?;For split assets such as dispatch skills, maintenance docs, architecture fragments, following-mol, command glossary, and TDD discipline content, is each resulting Core and Gastown output explicit?;Are review-marked assets resolved before downstream implementation depends on them?
personas.red_flags_text: large asset buckets are moved wholesale without behavior-based classification;specific review-marked assets remain unresolved;the migration table omits rationale or leaves orphaned duplicated behavior

personas.name: Mira Acharya
questions: Does each acceptance criterion map to a concrete unit, integration, command, or absence-test proof?
Are tests added or rewritten to fail if Core reintroduces Gastown role names while allowing documented migration exceptions and configurable dog defaults?
Are legacy Maintenance auto-inclusion tests rewritten to cover required Core plus explicit external Gastown imports?
red_flags: role-neutrality and behavior-preservation invariants rely on reviewer vigilance
legacy tests still assert implicit Maintenance inclusion
tests check only file presence instead of loaded behavior, resolution, or triggerability
personas.questions_text: Does each acceptance criterion map to a concrete unit, integration, command, or absence-test proof?;Are tests added or rewritten to fail if Core reintroduces Gastown role names while allowing documented migration exceptions and configurable dog defaults?;Are legacy Maintenance auto-inclusion tests rewritten to cover required Core plus explicit external Gastown imports?
personas.red_flags_text: role-neutrality and behavior-preservation invariants rely on reviewer vigilance;legacy tests still assert implicit Maintenance inclusion;tests check only file presence instead of loaded behavior, resolution, or triggerability

personas.name: Simone Kaye
personas.mandate: external Gastown pack authority;registry behavior;source-tree cleanliness;documentation consistency
questions: Does the requirement make gascity-packs/gastown the authoritative source for Gastown behavior, templates, overlays, and workflow checks?
Will no maintained pack source remain under examples/gastown/packs, and do docs and tutorials stop presenting retired locations as authoritative?
Are public registry behavior, template imports, and operator-facing terminology consistent across docs, CLI messages, and examples?
red_flags: external Gastown pack work is omitted while examples remain authoritative
documentation points operators at retired Maintenance or examples paths
template or registry behavior works locally but fails from public pack resolution

personas.red_flags_text: external Gastown pack work is omitted while examples remain authoritative;documentation points operators at retired Maintenance or examples paths;template or registry behavior works locally but fails from public pack resolution
